title: The cause of the error has been fixed: "NameError: name 'help' is not…
body:
… defined"

# Before submitting

- [-] Was this discussed/approved via a Github issue? (no need for typos, doc improvements)
- [+] Did you read the [contributor guideline](https://github.com/pytorch/fairseq/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)?
- [+] Did you make sure to update the docs?
- [+] Did you write any new necessary tests?

## What does this PR do?
A typo in the code causing an error in the operation of File "fairseq\dataclass\configs.py", line 1080, in EMAConfig has been fixed
NameError: name 'help' is not defined.

## PR review
Anyone in the community is free to review the PR once the tests have passed.
If we didn't discuss your PR in Github issues there's a high chance it will not be merged.

## Did you have fun?
Make sure you had fun coding 🙃

steps.operation: Fix dataclass field metadata key from an unquoted identifier to the expected string literal so the metadata dictionary is valid.
steps.reason: The field() metadata dict used `help` without quotes, which is treated as a variable name and raises `NameError: name 'help' is not defined` at import/runtime. Changing it to `"help"` matches the expected dataclass metadata convention and prevents the crash.
steps.side_effects: This affects how `EMAConfig.store_ema` exposes its help text to downstream config/CLI generation and documentation tooling; behavior should otherwise remain unchanged aside from eliminating the NameError during module import.

step_plan.summary: Fixes a NameError in `EMAConfig` by correcting a typo in the `store_ema` field metadata key (`help` -> `"help"`) so fairseq config code loads correctly.
